methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Calogena is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Frédéric Mariotte that has raised €100M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 11 nuclear energy peers: 3 publicly traded firms (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private companies including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

The peer-median method is not applicable here. The nuclear energy subsector lacks sufficient same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables—only 1 same-stage peer and 1 adjacent-stage peer were identified within the narrow filter, falling short of the minimum 3 datapoints required for reliable triangulation. Broadening to all 8 private-sector nuclear peers would introduce excessive heterogeneity in stage and valuation tier, undermining analytical precision.

Given this constraint, a defensible valuation requires alternative approaches not yet applied (such as discounted cash flow analysis on disclosed unit economics, if available, or revenue multiple analysis on disclosed financials). The current dataset does not support a quantified valuation range. Any forward valuation will depend critically on Calogena's path to cash flow breakeven, capital intensity relative to peers, and technology differentiation within advanced nuclear—variables not present in the provided data. Future analysis should prioritize obtaining financial metrics and technology specifications for the 5 leading private comparables.
